extraction.one_line_summary: EVENEPOEL Remco won from a 14-rider breakaway after riding strategically early, letting others work before dropping them near the finish.
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL forced into breakaway by Belgian press criticism, rode conservatively early to save energy versus typical steamrolling approach.
VINGEGAARD Jonas worked for KUSS Sepp on climbs, paying back domestique debt from earlier race support despite time gap.
KUSS used time bonuses to extend lead ensuring VINGEGAARD couldn't take red jersey even with sprint points remaining.
14-rider break succeeded because EVENEPOEL neutralized Jumbo-Visma's chase motivation by being present himself.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: responded to tactical criticism by racing intelligently rather than burning matches early, showing maturity under pressure
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: worked as domestique for KUSS despite being stronger GC rider, team cohesion overriding individual ambition
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: neutral: managed time bonuses carefully to maintain 17-second buffer over VINGEGAARD, racing defensively not offensively
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: positive: had power to enter early break despite Ineos struggling previous stages, legs improving in third week
rider_signals.MOSS Henrik: negative: attacked near finish but lacked punch to hold off EVENEPOEL despite late effort
